revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hine-jinja]][[ja:日根神社]] {{Expand Japanese|日根神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hine Shrine | native_name = 日根神社 | image = HineJinjaHaiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hine Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.374205|135.34267}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|cs|Ugajafukiaezu|en|Ugayafukiaezu|es|Ugayafukiaezu|fa|اوگایافوکی‌آئه‌زو|fr|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|ウガヤフキアエズ|ko|우가야후키아에즈|mni|ꯎꯒꯌꯐꯨꯀꯤꯑꯏꯓꯨ|pl|Ugayafukiaezu|ru|Угаяфукиаэдзу но Микото|simple|Ugayafukiaezu|th|อูงายาฟูกิอาเอซุ|uk|Уґаяфукі-аедзу но мікото|vi|Ugayafukiaezu|zh_yue|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|zh|鸕鷀草葺不合尊|qid=Q1373935|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} | website = {{Official website|https://hine-jinja.jp/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hine Shrine'''|日根神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Q134917301|ltq=Q134917301|qid=Q134917301|1=Go-no-Miya}} ** P1001: {{ill|Q1047117|ltq=Q1047117|qid=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver|lt=Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver|qid=Q135009152|1=Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * d'Izumi|gl|Provincia de Izumi|hak|Izumi-koet|id|Provinsi Izumi|it|Izumi (provincia)|ja|和泉国|ko|이즈미국|lt|Idzumio žemė|nl|Izumi (provincie)|pt|Província de Izumi|ru|Идзуми (провинция)|simple|Izumi Province|sk|Izumi (provincia)|sr|Провинција Изуми|tr|Izumi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Ідзумі|zh_min_nan|Izumi-kok|zh_yue|和泉國|zh|和泉國|qid=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}} * {{ill|Q11509546|ltq=Q11509546|qid=Q11509546|1=Hine district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.374205|135.34267}} == official website (P856) == * https://hine-jinja.jp/ ** Language of work: {{ill|Q5287|ltq=Q5287|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * HineJinjaHaiden.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q1373935|ltq=Q1373935|qid=Q1373935|1=Ugayafukiaezu}}https://hine-jinja.jp/privacy.html * {{ill|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|fa|تامایوری-هیمه|fr|Tamayori-bime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|mni|ꯇꯃꯌꯣꯔꯤ-ꯍꯤꯃꯦ|pl|Tamayori-bime|ru|Тамаёри-химэ|uk|Тамайорі хіме но мікото|zh|玉依姬|qid=Q3082751|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}}https://hine-jinja.jp/privacy.html == Kulturenvanteri monument ID (P11729) == * 415497 == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181015 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917326|ltq=Q134917326|qid=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == heritage designation (P1435) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ひねじんじゃ == Instagram username (P2003) == * hinejinja_official == Facebook username (P2013) == * hinejinja == adjacent building (P3032) == * {{ill|Jigen-in Temple|lt=Jigen-in Temple|gan|慈眼院|ja|慈眼院|qid=Q11495035|1=Jigen-in Temple}} == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 8150494 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8120105006360 == Commons category (P373) == * Hine-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府泉佐野市日根野631-1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hine-jinja}} [[JA:日根神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11509543}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Hine Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:HineJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|Hine Shrine haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumisano|Q852018|qid=Q852018|1=Izumisano}}{{ill|lt=Hineno Village|Q11509549|qid=Q11509549|lt=Hineno|1=Hineno}}631-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|qid=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime (Hyuga myth)|Q3082751|qid=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|qid=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|lt=Gonomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917326|lt=Fusha|1=Fu-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Oizeki Myojin, Oizeki Shrine |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 8|Q2555|qid=Q2555|1=May 8}} (procession rite) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hine Shrine''' (ひねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hineno|Q11509549|qid=Q11509549|1=Hineno}}, {{ill|lt=Izumisano|Q852018|qid=Q852018|1=Izumisano}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} in Hine District of {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|qid=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}}, and the fifth-ranked shrine (gonomiya) of Izumi Province. Its otabisho stands along the {{ill|lt=Kumano Kaido|Q11568976|qid=Q11568976|1=Kumano Kaidō}} (Kishu Kaido / Wakayama-Kaizuka Line) in {{ill|lt=Nagataki (Izumisano)|Q11653347|qid=Q11653347|lt=Nagataki|1=Nagataki}} in the same city. === History === The founding time is unclear. Shrine tradition says that during {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarebiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}'s eastern campaign, he fought {{ill|lt=Nagasunehiko|Q4922278|qid=Q4922278|1=Nagatsuhiko}} when entering {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}} from {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi|1=Kawachi Province}} and was defeated. Retreating to this field area, he enshrined the deity of "Hi" (heaven/day), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and the deity of "Ne" (the underworld), {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. After winning and pacifying Yamato, the area was named "Hineno" and the shrine became Hine Shrine. It is counted among the {{ill|lt=Izumi Five Shrines|Q11554710|qid=Q11554710|1=Izumi Inoue Shrine}} established in {{ill|lt=716|Q23531|qid=Q23531|1=716}} ({{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|qid=Q1373115|1=Reiki (era)}} 2), and is listed among the ten Engishiki shrines of Hine District, indicating a history of over 1200 years. In {{ill|lt=673|Q31105|qid=Q31105|1=673}} (Emperor {{ill|lt=Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} 2), the shrine-temple Muhenko-in (present {{ill|lt=Jigen-in|Q11495035|qid=Q11495035|1=Jigen-in Temple}}) was built adjacent to the precinct. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shomu|Q349263|qid=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, during the great drought of {{ill|lt=742|Q23601|qid=Q23601|1=742}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|qid=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 14), the shrine received 500 koku from the court. [[Image:HineJinjaHonden.jpg|right|200px|thumb|Hine Shrine main hall ({{ill|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|Q319531|qid=Q319531|lt=Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}})]] It was burned in warfare during the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era (1573-1593), and its lands were confiscated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} in 1585. In {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|qid=Q6727|1=1602}} ({{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7), shrine buildings were rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|qid=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}}. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era, it was separated from adjacent Muhenko-in by {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated as gosha; in {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|qid=Q2173|1=1924}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13), promoted to fūsha. The main hall was designated Osaka Prefecture tangible cultural property (Building No. 20) on March 31, {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|qid=Q2476|1=1972}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 47). {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oizeki Natural Park|1=UNKNOWN}} is adjacent. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|qid=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}}, {{ill|lt=Tamayorihime (Hyuga myth)|Q3082751|qid=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}}, four princes (Itsuse-no-Mikoto, Inahi-no-Mikoto, Mikenuma-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|lt=Wakamikenu-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}), Okishitomi-no-sukune === Precincts === [[Image:HimeJinja.jpg|right|200px|thumb|Hime Shrine]] * Main hall (Osaka Prefecture designated tangible cultural property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|qid=Q6727|1=1602}} (Keicho 7) by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Haiden - rebuilt in Keicho 7 (1602) by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Shinmon gate * Shrine office - formerly Shimonobo, a sub-temple of Muhenko-in (present {{ill|lt=Jigen-in|Q11495035|qid=Q11495035|1=Jigen-in Temple}}). * Hime Shrine (Osaka Prefecture designated tangible cultural property) - deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Ohirume no Muchi no Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. A Shikinaisha. Listed as official shrine in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 1) under Emperor {{ill|lt=Seiwa|Q349243|qid=Q349243|1=Emperor Seiwa}}. * Niu Shrine (Nonomiya) - deities: Niutsuhime Omikami (Wakahirume-no-Mikoto), Niu Koya Miko Omikami ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kariba Myojin|1=UNKNOWN}}). Said to have been invited in Tenpyo 30 of Emperor Shomu. Original shrine is {{ill|lt=Niu Tsuhime Shrine|Q705154|qid=Q705154|lt=Niu Tsuhime Shrine|1=Niutsuhime Shrine}} (Amano Taisha) in Kamiamano, Katsuragi, Ito District, {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|qid=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}. Festival held October 10. * {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|qid=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu|1=Ebisu (mythology)}}-miya (sub-shrine of Niu Shrine) - deity: {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} no Mikoto. Origins unclear; records mention rename after transfer in Tenpo 11 and merger of three deities into Niu Shrine at current location in Meiji 41. * Okamae Shrine * Otabisho File:HineShrineOtabisho.JPG|Hine Shrine otabisho. {{Coord|34.3888|135.3096|format=dms|dscale:30000}} === Festivals === * Makura Festival (May 5) === Cultural properties === === National Historic Site === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hine-no-sho ruins|1=UNKNOWN}} - this shrine is included as one component. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main hall * Hime Shrine building === Osaka City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Hine Shrine Makura Festival (Hine Shrine Makura Festival Preservation Society) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://hine-jinja.jp Hine Shrine] (official site) * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai12shibu/izumisano-city/12030hinejinja.html Hine Shrine] (Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Commons|Category:Hine-jinja|Hine Shrine}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hine Shrine]][[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Go-no-Miya]] [[Category:Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Izumisano, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumisano, Osaka]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]]
